title: proboscidea parviflora subsp. gracillima
intro: Proboscidea parviflora subsp. gracillima, also known as the Mexican horsemint or Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species in the bignonia family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea, tincture, or salve, and has been used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds.
